doc.name: doc
doc.title: Texas Health and Human Services Commission SNAP current-effective handbook and bulletin extracts

hcontainer.name: section
hcontainer.heading: SNAP Income Limits, Deductions, and Allotments: standard utility allowance
hcontainer.remark: Source: MEPD and TW Bulletin 25-15; published 2025-09-05; effective 2025-10-01.

intro.p: Standard or Maximum Deduction Amounts (Effective Oct. 1, 2025)

content.p: Use the following updated deduction amounts:

hcontainer.name: section
hcontainer.heading: SNAP Income Limits, Deductions, and Allotments: homeless shelter deduction
hcontainer.remark: Source: MEPD and TW Bulletin 25-15; published 2025-09-05; effective 2025-10-01.

intro.p: Standard or Maximum Deduction Amounts (Effective Oct. 1, 2025)

content.p: Use the following updated deduction amounts:
Allowable child support payments may be in the form of cash support, medical support, or payments to third parties.
To be an allowable deduction, these payments must be ordered by a court or administrative authority and be equal to or less than the household's child support obligation.
A child support deduction for households that pay legally obligated child support is allowed.
Allowable self-employment expenses are based on costs that can be deducted from federal income taxes on the Internal Revenue Service's Schedule C, Form 1040.
Certain self-employment expense types are not allowed for SNAP.
Determine the total allowable expenses, then subtract the total allowable expenses from the total gross self-employment income to determine net self-employment income.
SNAP households meet categorical eligibility criteria if all members are approved for TANF cash assistance or SSI.

SNAP households also meet categorical eligibility criteria if the household meets the resource criteria to be authorized to receive TANF Non-cash (TANF-NC) services and has gross income less than or equal to 165 percent of the Federal Poverty Income Limit (FPIL) for its size.
Categorical eligibility extends to any household authorized to receive services funded by the TANF program.
The TANF non-cash (TANF-NC) program offers various services such as family planning, adult education, prevention and treatment of substance abuse, and employment services.
Households must pass an income and resource test for determination of categorical eligibility based on receipt of TANF-NC services.
To meet the resource test, the household's countable liquid resources plus excess vehicle value must be $5,000 or less.
